meta.title: Choei Yuda Ski Area
meta.description: Private-feel Japow powder and Yuda Onsenkyo—adult day ticket ¥2,130 at a hidden micro-resort.

hero.title:
Yuda
Ski Area
hero.tagline: Deep snow with almost no crowds—and legendary tofu at Rest House Yunosawa in the Ou Mountains.

transit.title:
7 minutes from Yuda IC,
bus from Hotto-Yuda Station.
transit.lead: About 7–15 minutes by car from the Yuda IC on the Akita Expressway, or 7–10 minutes by bus from JR Kitakami Line Hotto-Yuda Station—unusually good access for a deep-snow local hill. A second choice for riders avoiding crowds at larger resorts.
transit.imgAlt: Snow road in Yuda Onsenkyo—access to the ski hill

primary.title: Private-feel premium powder
primary.lead: Low visitation means untracked zones and deep snow when timing aligns—a premium experience for powder hunters skipping crowded, pricey mega-resorts.
primary.imgAlt: Deep powder at Yuda Ski Area—near-private laps

secondary.title: Carving heaven on Course A
secondary.lead: Open views and moderate pitch—ideal for carving repetition. Up to ~820 m run length and 34° max pitch on a serious municipal hill.

pass.title:
Adults ¥2,130,
half-day ¥1,250.
pass.lead: Nationwide-bargain pricing—day ticket ¥2,130, half-day ¥1,250. Built for core riders who want focused short sessions. Buy lift tickets at the Rest House Yunosawa cafeteria counter.
pass.noticeBody: There is no dedicated ticket booth—buy at the Yunosawa cafeteria counter before the hill. First-timers: follow on-site signage.
pass.imgAlt: Yuda Ski Area—serious skiing at low cost

rental.title:
Ski, eat,
soak in one day.
rental.lead: Morning half-day laps for powder and carving, lunch with tofu steak at Yunosawa, afternoon at Sunayukko sand bath or Hotto-Yuda onsen—a Yuda Onsenkyo loop that lifts spend per visitor.
rental.whisper: No official rental on site—hands-free packs via local lodgings are a growth lever (report recommendation).
rental.imgAlt: Yuda Onsenkyo—pairing skiing with hot springs

yukinko.title:
Yunosawa
tofu steak.
yukinko.lead: Rest House Yunosawa at the base—famous tofu steak set using Gen-suke cotton tofu and natto-jiru ramen. Quality worth a visit even without skiing.

family.title:
Hotto-Yuda &
Sunayukko.
family.lead: Minutes away: Kawajiri Onsen Hotto-Yuda (station with baths and train signal lights) and Tsuzusawa Onsen Sunayukko (Tōhoku's first day-trip sand bath)—perfect après detox.
family.noticeBody: Morning ski → Yunosawa lunch → afternoon onsen—target ¥5,000–7,000 per person town spend (report recommendation).

workation.title:
First tracks AM,
sand bath PM.
workation.lead: Half-day ticket 9:00–12:00 for fresh-track powder, then Sunayukko sand bath for deep detox—the Powder & Detox one-day trip.

01.title: To Yuda IC
01.body: ~7 min by car from Yuda IC

local.title: Private powder × Yuda Onsenkyo guide
local.body: Position as a second choice to crowded mega-resorts—low price, silence, deep snow. Tickets at Yunosawa cafeteria, no rental—honest disclosure for core riders (report ch. 5).

schools.title: Rest House Yunosawa synergy
schools.body: Hill and cafeteria operate as one—tofu cuisine draws food-only visitors. Digital ticket redemption without blocking kitchen ops (report ch. 6).

summer.title: Lake Kinshuko & green season
summer.body: Kinshuko Lake canoeing/SUP, Yunosawa campground—year-round potential on existing nature assets (report ch. 6).

access.note: ~7–15 min by car from Yuda IC · ~7–10 min bus from JR Hotto-Yuda Station
